'''Sam Walters''' (born 25 December 2000) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]].

He has spent time on loan from [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at the [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Walters played his amateur rugby league with the Halton Farnworth Hornets

==Career==
===Leeds Rhinos===
Walters made his Super League début in round 14 of the [[Super League XXV results|2020 Super League season]] for Leeds against the [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/leeds-vs-catalans/teams/54288|title=Starting Lineups - Leeds vs Catalans|publisher=Sky Sports |date=30 September 2020}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/1587/round-14-fixtures-confirmed|title=Round 14 fixtures confirmed|publisher=Sky Sports |date=29 September 2020}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos-6-catalans-dragons-34-youthful-rhinos-impress-spirited-display-against-dragons-2988868|title=Leeds Rhinos 6 Catalans Dragons 34: Youthful Rhinos impress in spirited display against Dragons |publisher=Yorkshire Evening Post |first=Peter |last=Smith |date=30 September 2020}}</ref>

Walters has also played one game for [[Batley Bulldogs|Batley]] whilst out on loan where he scored against [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]].

On 24 September 2022, Walters played for Leeds in their 24-12 loss to [[St Helens RFC]] in the [[2022 Super League Grand Final]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2022/sep/24/st-helens-leeds-super-league-grand-final-match-report-rugby-league/|title=St Helens sink Leeds to win record fourth consecutive Grand Final|newspaper=The Observer|date=24 September 2022 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }}</ref>

Walters played 17 matches for Leeds in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished 8th on the table and missed the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos-2023-season-review-picking-out-the-best-and-worst-players-performances-results-4355920/|title=Leeds Rhinos' 2023 season review: picking out the best and worst players, performances, results|publisher=Yorkshire Evening Post}}</ref>

===Wigan Warriors===
Ahead of the 2024 season, Walters joined Wigan Warriors on a three year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://wiganwarriors.com/blog/2023/06/26/wigan-sign-sam-walters-from-2024/ |title=Wigan sign Sam Walters from 2024 |website=Wigan Warriors |date=26 June 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231022072713/https://www.wiganwarriors.com/blog/2023/06/26/wigan-sign-sam-walters-from-2024/ |archive-date=22 October 2023}}</ref> 

In June 2025, he signed a new four year deal with the club.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/articles/c8xve8g7vwpo |title=Wigan forward Walters signs contract until 2029 |work=BBC Sport |date=18 June 2025 |access-date=13 May 2026}}</ref> On 9 October 2025, Walters played in Wigan's 24-6 2025 Super League Grand Final loss against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2jw497jpzo/|title=Hull KR beat Wigan in Grand Final to complete treble|work=BBC Sport}}</ref>
On 30 May 2026, Walters played in Wigan's 2026 Challenge Cup final victory against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2026/jun/01/challenge-cup-crowds-are-dwindling-but-rugby-league-must-save-wembley-relationship|title=Challenge Cup crowds are dwindling but rugby league must save Wembley relationship|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>
